connectorUiConfig.title: Trend Micro Cloud App Security (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The [Trend Micro Cloud App Security](https://www.trendmicro.com/en_be/business/products/user-protection/sps/email-and-collaboration/cloud-app-security.html) data connector provides the capability to retrieve security event logs of the services that Cloud App Security protects into Microsoft Sentinel through the SIEM API. The connector provides the ability to get events which helps to examine potential security risks, analyze your team's use of collaboration, diagnose configuration problems and more. It supports DCR-based [ingestion time transformations](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/custom-logs-overview) that parse the received security event data into custom columns so that queries don't need to parse it again, thus resulting in better performance.

sampleQueries.description: Get sample of recent security events

dataTypes.name: TrendMicroCASV2
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
TrendMicroCASV2
| where TimeGenerated > ago(6h)
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

customs.name: Trend Micro CAS API Token
customs.description: A Trend Micro Cloud App Security API token is required. [See the documentation to learn more about Trend Micro CAS API](https://docs.trendmicro.com/enterprise/cloud-app-security-integration-api-online-help/supported-cloud-app-/log-retrieval-api/get-security-logs.aspx).

instructionSteps.title: 1. Configure Trend Micro CAS Data Streams
instructionSteps.description: Add one or more data streams by selecting the cloud service and event type combinations you want to monitor.

parameters.label: Add Data Stream
parameters.title: Add Trend Micro CAS Stream
parameters.subtitle: Configure a new service and event type combination

parameters.content:
## Configuration

Configure the connection to your Trend Micro Cloud App Security instance and select which service and event type to monitor.

parameters.label: API Token
parameters.placeholder: Enter your Trend Micro CAS API Token
parameters.name: apiToken

parameters.text: The API token will be securely stored and used for authentication with the Trend Micro CAS API.

parameters.content:
## Stream Configuration

Select the cloud service and event type for this data stream.

parameters.label: Friendly Name
parameters.placeholder: Descriptive name combining service and event type (e.g., 'SharePoint-Ransomware')
parameters.name: friendlyName

parameters.text: Each data stream configuration creates a dedicated poller for the selected service and event type combination. You can add multiple streams as needed.

instructionSteps.title: 2. Migrate from legacy Azure Function connector (optional)
instructionSteps.description:
Use this section if you are moving from the legacy Trend Micro CAS Azure Function connector to this CCF connector.

### Migration summary

- The legacy connector used Azure Function app settings such as `WorkspaceID`, `WorkspaceKey`, `TrendMicroCASToken`, and `TrendMicroCASServiceURL`.
- This CCF connector uses connector stream configuration fields instead (`serviceURL`, `apiToken`, `service`, `eventType`, and `friendlyName`) and writes to the `TrendMicroCASV2` table.
- Existing detections and hunting content should query the `TrendMicroCAS` parser alias so both legacy and CCF data remain supported during transition.

parameters.content:
### Recommended migration steps

1. Keep the legacy connector running while you configure at least one CCF data stream.
2. In **Step 1**, add streams for each service/event type pair you currently ingest.
3. Verify new events appear in the CCF table:

```kusto
TrendMicroCASV2
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| take 20
```

4. Verify normalized parser output still returns expected data:

```kusto
TrendMicroCAS
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| take 20
```

5. After validation, disable or remove the legacy Azure Function connector to avoid duplicate ingestion and cost.

### Troubleshooting

If no CCF data appears:
- Confirm the API token is valid and has permission to read security events.
- Confirm the service URL is correct (for example, `api.tmcas.trendmicro.com`).
- Check that selected **Cloud Service** and **Event Type** values match what your tenant generates.
- Wait a few polling cycles and rerun the validation query.
